behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/aggregation (collectors -> composer). MOCK-MISS: this is not a single-function pure-logic check; it is cross-module wiring. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real network is not required to catch the core failure; stubbed source clients can expose missing/overwritten sections. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no dependence on model judgment in this assertion. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires inclusion of data in payload, not real HTTP/email/model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/composition modules with three deterministic source stubs, execute one run, and assert the summary-input payload contains sections/fields from metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log together.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure is error propagation across modules (source client failure -> report builder fallback). MOCK-MISS: unit tests of fallback formatter alone miss orchestration behavior. REAL-INFRA: outage can be simulated via stub exception/timeout result; real network is not required for minimum failure detection. LLM-DEP: correctness here is fallback behavior, not model judgment. Rejected higher level: text specifies unavailable source handling, not real infrastructure behavior under OS/network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline with two normal source stubs and one failing stub; assert process completes, output includes `data unavailable` for the failed section, and no global abort occurs.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: This is a quality claim about generated narrative correctness. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model output content (faithfulness), and a stub would mask hallucination/omission failures (deterministic-assertion trap). MULTI-STEP: single model judgment step, not a multi-agent sequence. Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly requires 'accurately represents' content quality, which integration wiring tests cannot validate.
behaviors.test_description: Use representative real input datasets and call the real summary model/generator; evaluate whether key numeric/status facts are preserved in output (with rubric or semantic checks) across repeated runs/tolerance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA boundary is explicit: end-to-end delivery to a real inbox requires real SMTP/API + network behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked mail transport can pass while real auth/connection/provider handling fails. LLM-DEP: behavior checks delivery completion, not summary quality; no explicit requirement to validate model decisions. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: text focuses on transport outcome ('reaches inbox correctly'), not multi-agent quality or sequenced model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled/report script in an environment using real source endpoints (or realistic test instances) and real email delivery to a test mailbox; assert message arrival, recipient correctness, and expected report structure/content presence.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is state handoff across local components/runs: delivery failure -> local persistence -> later retry consumption. This can be caught with real modules plus temp filesystem and controlled mail client failure/success stubs. REAL-INFRA check: no mandatory real external email outage or cron daemon needed for minimum detection; two controlled invocations simulate scheduled runs. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not model-quality dependent. Rejected System: behavior text requires retry semantics, not validation of real provider/network edge conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run once with email client forced to fail; assert report persisted to local retry file/queue. Run again with email client succeeding; assert persisted report is sent and retry artifact is cleared/marked delivered.
